name: Willis Brown
description: A resident and longtime small business owner with deep knowledge of the local economy and mentoring experience with young entrepreneurs.
source_text: Willis Brown is a resident and longtime small business owner on Euclid Avenue. He has deep knowledge of the local economy, relationships with the Chamber of Commerce, and has mentored several young entrepreneurs in the city.

name: Andre Brice
description: A resident who runs an informal job readiness program connecting unemployed residents with local employers.
source_text: Andre Brice runs an informal job readiness program out of his home, connecting unemployed residents with local employers. He has placed over 30 people in jobs in the last two years.

name: Cheryl Mays
description: A resident and business owner who operates a catering business and regularly donates meals to community events.
source_text: Cheryl Mays is both a resident and business owner. She operates a catering business that sources food from local suppliers and regularly donates meals to community events.

name: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity - Euclid Chapter
description: An active fraternal organization with community service programming including scholarship funds and youth mentorship.
source_text: Kappa Alpha Psi fraternity has a chapter in Euclid with active community service programming including scholarship funds and youth mentorship.

name: Kiwanis Club of Euclid
description: A long-established service organization with 30 years of organizing community projects and strong institutional connections.
source_text: Kiwanis Club of Euclid has been organizing community service projects and fundraisers for 30 years, with strong institutional connections to local schools and government.

name: Juneteenth Block Party Coalition
description: A coalition of Black-owned businesses and residents that has organized an annual celebration for 12 years.
source_text: Euclid's annual Juneteenth block party, organized by a coalition of Black-owned businesses and residents, is a 12-year tradition that celebrates African American heritage and spotlights local entrepreneurs.

name: Euclid Hunger Center
description: A formal organization providing food assistance and nutrition resources to families across the city.
source_text: The Euclid Hunger Center provides food assistance and nutrition resources to families across the city.

name: Pope's Hardware
description: A family-owned business that donates tools and materials to neighborhood improvement projects.
source_text: Pope's Hardware on Euclid Avenue has been family-owned for three generations and donates tools and materials to neighborhood improvement projects.

name: Cheryl Mays' Catering Business
description: A local catering business that sources from local suppliers and contributes food to community events.
source_text: She operates a catering business that sources food from local suppliers and regularly donates meals to community events.

name: Willis Brown's Small Business
description: A longtime small business on Euclid Avenue that provides local employment and economic knowledge.
source_text: Willis Brown is a resident and longtime small business owner on Euclid Avenue. He has deep knowledge of the local economy, relationships with the Chamber of Commerce, and has mentored several young entrepreneurs in the city.

name: Pope's Hardware - Family Business
description: A three-generation family-owned business providing local employment and economic activity.
source_text: Pope's Hardware on Euclid Avenue has been family-owned for three generations and donates tools and materials to neighborhood improvement projects.

name: Juneteenth Celebration
description: A 12-year tradition celebrating African American heritage and community identity.
source_text: Euclid's annual Juneteenth block party, organized by a coalition of Black-owned businesses and residents, is a 12-year tradition that celebrates African American heritage and spotlights local entrepreneurs.

name: Black-Owned Business Community
description: Local entrepreneurs and business owners who maintain cultural heritage and community identity through their enterprises.
source_text: Euclid's annual Juneteenth block party, organized by a coalition of Black-owned businesses and residents, is a 12-year tradition that celebrates African American heritage and spotlights local entrepreneurs.
